description: Decision rules used by the local Ollama planner to behave like an experienced Excel analyst. The planner consults these rules to translate phrasing into the right action and to decide when a single command is enough versus when a multi-step plan is needed. Rules are advisory — when a rule and a clarification need conflict, prefer to ask for clarification.

by_phrase_routing.description: Phrases like 'by program', 'by major', 'by department', 'by advisor' indicate grouped operations.

chart_rules.description: Charts always need a structured table before the chart command. If user asks for a chart without first specifying numeric metric, prefer count_rows.
rules: Build the summary table first (sum_by_group, count_by_group, or average_column) and place it on its own sheet.
Then create_chart from that sheet, never from the raw enrollment sheet directly when a summary is needed.
If user says 'trend', use line chart and require a time/semester column.

report_rules.description: Reports are multi-step plans by default.
default_plan: create_chart (optional, only if user asked for visualization or it is a standard playbook)

formula_rules.description: Excel formula choice rules for create_formula.

safety_rules.description: Hard rules the planner must never violate.
rules: Never propose to delete original data by default — use move_rows_to_sheet (copy) instead of deleting.
Never overwrite the original workbook — output_sheet is always a new sheet.
Never invent column names — only use columns present in available_column_names_by_sheet.
Never invent sheet names — only use names in available_sheet_names.
Never include personally identifiable data in the plan output — refer to columns by name only.
Always set requires_confirmation to true unless plan_type is clarify.

clean_up_rule.description: When the user says 'clean it up' or 'clean this up', the planner must ask which kind of cleanup they want.
